title: Yen falls to 162 per dollar, its lowest level in 39 and a half years
summary: The Japanese currency weakens against the dollar amid diverging monetary policies between Japan and the US, while the market awaits possible currency intervention.

title: Japan eliminated from World Cup after 2-1 loss to Brazil in round of 16
summary: The Japanese team took the lead with a goal from Sano, but Brazil came back in the second half and sealed it at the end, leaving Japan out of the next round.

title: Germany, four-time champion, eliminated from World Cup on penalties by Paraguay
summary: The German team fell in the penalty shootout in their round of 16 match, a surprising defeat for one of the favorites.

title: Keiko Fujimori wins Peruvian presidential election
summary: The candidate of Fuerza Popular prevails in the second round and becomes the country's first female president, according to official projections.

title: US Supreme Court upholds sexual abuse conviction against Donald Trump
summary: The high court dismissed the former president's appeal, confirming a $8.3 million compensation to writer E. Jean Carroll.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 1,700 dead and massive damage
summary: The quake, one of the deadliest in the country's history, destroyed tens of thousands of buildings, according to NASA satellite images.

title: US and Iran agree to hold talks in Qatar on Gulf security
summary: Both countries will send delegations to Doha to discuss a possible cessation of hostilities affecting oil traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Six dead in a shooting at a shelter for mothers and children in Germany
summary: The attacker, with a history of child custody disputes, opened fire at a residence in the north of the country.

title: Kintetsu train derails at Kyoto station without causing injuries
summary: The accident occurred on a curve near the station, affecting about 85,000 passengers; services resumed after repairs.

title: Japanese government finalizes details to reform the Imperial Household Law
summary: Deputy Prime Minister Aso and Chief Cabinet Secretary Fujita are in final talks to present a bill that would allow male adoption in the imperial family.

title: Lower House committee approves seat reduction bill without opposition
summary: The opposition boycotted the session, but the committee chairman used his authority to start the debate; a plenary vote is expected.

title: Japanese public servants receive average summer bonus of over 730,000 yen
summary: The amount for non-managerial employees increased compared to the previous year, according to the government.

title: Average income of Japanese lawmakers was 30.03 million yen in 2024
summary: Financial disclosures reveal that ruling party president Takaichi was the highest paid among party leaders.

title: Japan's industrial output rises 0.5% in May driven by transportation
summary: The transport machinery sector led the growth, but the government maintains a moderate outlook due to global uncertainty.

title: Nikkei index rises more than 1,000 points and recovers the 70,000 level
summary: Optimism in the US technology sector and the yen's weakness boosted Japan's main stock index.

title: Dow Jones closes at record 52,000 points for the first time
summary: The inclusion of Alphabet in the index and purchases of technology stocks drove the industrial average to a new all-time high.

title: Japan keeps the name 'sansuu' for mathematics in elementary school
summary: The Ministry of Education decided not to unify the name with secondary school, after a debate in which the conservative position prevailed.

title: Netherlands takes the lead against Morocco in World Cup
summary: The Dutch team scored in the second half of their round of 16 match, although the final result is not detailed in the reports.

title: Japan's unemployment rate remains at 2.5% in May and job availability slightly decreases
summary: The unemployment rate remained stable, while the jobs-to-applicants ratio fell one-tenth to 1.17, according to government data.

title: US Supreme Court limits police access to mobile phone location data
summary: The court ruled that obtaining location data without a warrant constitutes an illegal search, protecting digital privacy.
